page: Microsoft Azure D8ls_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the DLSv5 family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, with 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The server is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This low-memory configuration positions the instance primarily for compute-focused workloads that do not require massive RAM footprints.

Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the D8ls_v5 does not include any bundled local storage, though it is Premium Storage capable. There are no integrated GPUs. The Intel Xeon processor includes a 48 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, AVX-512 Vector Neural Network Instructions, and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

Benchmark data places the D8ls_v5 consistently in the average performance tier across most metrics. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1674.0 and a multi-core score of 6230.0, alongside a PassMark CPU score of 11077.41. Memory bandwidth tests show 57.69 GB/sec for uncached large block reads and 38.67 GB/sec for writes. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is a relative bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% tier. However, the instance demonstrates solid capabilities for static web serving at 12.59 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 8.08 million operations per second. This profile makes the server suitable for compute-oriented tasks such as software compilation, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference using small models.
description: Microsoft Azure D8ls_v5 is an x86_64 compute-focused server from the DLSv5 family, featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz with 4 physical cores, it operates under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance lacks bundled local storage and GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. It supports AVX-512 and cryptographic accelerations like AES and SHA-NI. Performance benchmarks generally fall into the average tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6230.0 and Redis SET performance of 8.08 million operations per second. The server is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads with low memory requirements, making it suitable for software compilation, web serving, and small-scale model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8ls_v5 is a compute-focused x86_64 server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and no bundled local storage, optimized for web serving and software compilation.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8ls_v5 is an 8-vCPU, 16.0 GB RAM compute-focused server powered by an Intel Xeon processor, ideal for web serving and compilation.
tagline: A compute-focused, low-memory virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and AVX-512 support for efficient, CPU-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Premium Storage capable configuration with no bundled local storage
Supports AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and lightweight inference
